details.text: Derivative: فَاطِرِ from فَطَرَ to cleave, break or split apart but also to bring into being and to create.
Derivative: الْعِزَّةَ means power, respect and honour.
Derivative: Means to continually decrease and windle until it is nothing and yields no benefit.

إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ الْكَلِمُ الطَّيِّبُ وَالْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ يَرْفَعُهُ

- “To him rises (يَصْعَدُ) the good words/speech (الْكَلِمُ الطَّيِّبُ) and the righteous act raises it or He raises it” – This has been interpreted with two meanings. 1) That the good words are raised to Him and the righteous act is (also) raised to Him. 2) That the good words are raised towards Him, in the case where يَصْعَدُ implies climbing upwards towards something, and the righteous act then lifts it up i.e., reinforces the good word and boosts it.
Derivative: مَوَاخِرَ means to plough through the water, describing how ships plough, cut or tear across the sea, its waters and waves.
Derivative: سَائِغٌ means to go down easily, to be pleasant, agreeable and palatable. It is the opposite of غَصَّ which means to choke and be congested i.e., where something does not go down easily.
Derivative: فُرَاتٌ means extremely or intensely sweet.
Derivative: Sweet and fresh.

Derivative: قِطْمِيرٍ refers to the shred pealed from a date stone. The date is the smallest item of value in Arabic societies. The stone of the date may have some value because you can plant it. However, a fragment pealed from the stone of the date is completely worthless. That is قِطْمِيرٍ. It is similar to the usage of the word نَقِير which refers to a spot or tiny string attached to a date stone and is also an expression for something that is completely worthless or something that is miniscule.
- Literally: “You are the impoverished/poor (الْفُقَرَاءُ) to Allah” which means you are the ones in need of Allah. To be فَقِير (poor) to Allah means to be in need of Him and dependent upon Him.
Derivative: يُذْهِبْ from أَذْهَبَ when used with بِ means to get rid of, do away with or make disappear/be gone.

- When مَا عَلَيْهِ بِعَزِيز comes stated, it means: “It is no big deal or matter for Him” i.e., “It is easy for Him to accomplish without effort”, or “It is nothing for Him”, or “It is not at all hard or difficult for Him”.
Derivative: مُثْقَلَةٌ is someone who is heavily burdened with sins.

- “By the unseen” means either that they fear Allah even though they don’t see him, or, that they fear Allah in that which is unseen (by others), where they fear Him in that which is secret and what is public.
Derivative: الْحَرُورُ from حَرَّ – حَرَارَة and refers to severe heat.
Derivative: خَلَا – يَخْلُو means to already have happened, to pass by (in time) or through.
Derivative: الزُّبُرِ refers to scriptures and books that were revealed.
Derivative: نَكِيرِ refers to the condemnation upon them (the disbelievers) and its associated terrible destruction and punishment.
Derivative: غَرَابِيبُ is the plural of غِرْبِيب which refers to the intense blackness of a crow (غُراب). Its meaning is jet black where no colour other than black can be seen.

- This is an example of what is called النَعْتُ السَّبَبِي (An-Na’t As-Sababiy) where the adjective (النَّعْت or صِفَّة) follows what proceeded it in terms of being definite or indefinite without regard for what follows it.
- It can also be referred to as Mubtada’ and Khabar where the Khabar precedes the Mubtada’, in the case where the meaning is أَلْوَانُهَا مُخْتَلِفَة “The colours of which are different or varied”.
Derivative: تُؤْفَكُونَ means to be diverted and turned away i.e., away from belief and the truth. أَفَكَ refers to any diversion away from what the truth is upon.
Derivative: يُوَفِّيَ to pay, repay or recompense in full.

Derivative: شَكُورٌ is used for Allah and also for His slaves. For His slaves شَكُورٌ (Shakoor) means extremely grateful and thanking Allah a lot. For Allah, شَكُورٌ refers to His acknowledgement, appreciation and acceptance of His slaves’ efforts and acts of obedience.
Derivative: مُّقْتَصِدٌ refers to someone who takes a moderate path i.e., restricts himself to undertaking what is obligatory and leaving what is prohibited.
Derivative: لُغُوبٌ is the exhaustion and tiredness resulting from being overworked.
Derivative: نَصَبٌ means to be exhausted and fatigues. It is said that it means to be so tired that you can no longer stand up.

Derivative: الْمُقَامَةِ is the place in which the إِقامَة lasts continuously. It is the اِسْم الظَّرْف from أَقَامَ – يُقِيمُ (to reside) which is مُقَام with the additional ة (Taa’ Al-Marbootah) which provides the meaning of permanency i.e., الْمُقَامَةِ is permanent residency.
Derivative: كَفُورٍ means someone who is extremely ungrateful or someone who is extreme in his denial and disbelief i.e., of the signs and favours of Allah.
Derivative: مَقْتًا is great hatred and dislike. In the language it is a hatred attached to disgust towards something which is despicable and intolerable.
Derivative: خَلَائِفَ is the plural of خَلِيفَة and means those who leave other behind i.e., you will be successors of each other, one generation after another.

Derivative: نُفُورًا means aversion i.e., to turn away from something with animosity and hatred. In this context “They are not increased in anything but نُفُورًا” it also means that their aversion to the truth and guidance increases in obstinacy and becomes further entrenched within them.
- “They swear by Allah their strongest oaths” – جَهْدَ refers to effort and making strenuous pains i.e., they go over the top in their oaths and make a big show of them.
Derivative: تَحْوِيلًا means an alteration or change. In the course it refers to a change or alteration in its course or direction, which could also imply deviation or diversion.

- It literally means: “The evil plotting does not encompass except its own people (i.e., those who made the plotting)”. It means that their plotting rebounds to them and is similar to the English Phrase: “What goes around, comes around” i.e., you will inevitably reap the consequences of your bad actions.
details.arabic_part: لَا يَحِيقُ الْمَكْرُ السَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِ

details.text: Derivative: يُعْجِزَ means to make or render something incapable, to diminish something’s capabilities, to disable and cripple them. It can be understood to mean to overpower.
Derivative: قَدِيرًا refers to full capability and also to someone who possesses a complete or flawless calculation.
Derivative: بَصِيرًا – When Allah calls Himself بَصِير “To have a complete and full view” it refers to both what is in the inside and what is on the outside.
Derivative: تَغُرَّنَّ from غَرَّ – يَغُرُّ and it means to be deceived, deluded, duped or conned. It has also been said that غَرَّ implies being deceived where you discover the deception at the end.
Derivative: الْغَرُورُ “The deceiver” referring to Shaytan.
Derivative: تُثِيرُ from أَثَارَ – إِثَارَة which means to raise, stimulate and stir up.
